steps.title: Factor the second equation
steps.content: The second equation is $y = 8x - 24$. Notice that the right side can be factored: $$8x - 24 = 8(x - 3)$$ So the system becomes: $$y = (x-3)(x+5)$$ $$y = 8(x-3)$$

steps.title: Set equations equal and factor
steps.content: Since both expressions equal $y$, set them equal: $$(x-3)(x+5) = 8(x-3)$$ Move everything to one side and factor out the common term $(x-3)$: $$(x-3)(x+5) - 8(x-3) = 0$$ $$(x-3)[(x+5) - 8] = 0$$ $$(x-3)(x - 3) = 0$$ Therefore $x = 3$.

steps.title: Substitute to find y and identify the solution
steps.content: Substitute $x = 3$ into the second equation: $$y = 8(3) - 24 = 24 - 24 = 0$$ The solution is the ordered pair $(3, 0)$. That confirms C is correct.
